json_data: Individual Task 2 for WID2003 Cognitive Science, an individual assignment on Cognitive Architecture, is due March 30, 2026, via MS Teams, with lectures on March 19 and 24, 2026, and requires a written report (max 5 pages) for Parts 1, 2, 4, and 5, supported by recommended readings and videos.

The assignment aims to develop students' ability to explain key ideas from cognitive science and AI foundations, reflect on personal thinking, and connect theoretical concepts to real-world experiences.
Parts 1 and 2 of the task focus on engaging with foundational cognitive science concepts, defining cognitive architecture, and exploring its various models (e.g., symbolic, subsymbolic, hybrid).

Part 3 is an exploratory section, not requiring a written report, which focuses on the purpose and structure of ACT-R as a computational tool, with other cognitive architectures mentioned including SOAR, CLARION, LIDA, EPIC, NARS, ICARUS, and Sigma.

Parts 4 and 5 require summarizing an application of cognitive architecture, such as Intelligent Tutoring Systems or Human-Robot Interaction, and comparing cognitive architectures with rule-based and deep learning AI systems, including their strengths, limitations, and Explainable AI (XAI) aspects.
